title: calamagrostis nana subsp. nana
intro: Calamagrostis nana subsp. nana, also known as Dwarf Brome, is a type of small grass that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to the mountainous regions of Central and Eastern Europe. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be utilized to treat wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Calamagrostis nana subsp. nana include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
